reviews.review_msg: Lectures were taken from a course done at Yale (actually very interesting). Prof had smth going on this semester but grades were not given back within a reasonable time and I had no idea how I was doing in the class for most of the semester. Way too many readings for the expected time based on credits.

Craig is a good professor, and a smart guy. Don't get me wrong, the texts and documentaries he provided were interesting. There is just a lot. And I mean, a lot! There is no lecture, but still, I feel as if there wasn't much care given to just how much was being demanded of student's time here.

Doesn't actually lecture. VERY reading intensive (~300 pages/week). Makes you watch 3 hours worth of video content because he enjoys watching movies (so apparently you have to as well). Doesn't even really answer emails on time.

Take this class if you have nothing else to do with your time.

I would like to preface this by saying I definitely did not give this professor a fair shake. However very early he dropped a assignment. I cant go into the full story because of word limit, but it was a poorly executed assignment which I interpreted as a red flag. Some questions you just could not answer because of their nature.

No lecture videos, no lectures but 2-3 videos (1 hour long each) and 150-200 pages of readings per week. PER WEEK. It definitely felt like I paid $1,000 to be stressed out and watch movies and read. Tests are based on the nitty-gritty details. I still don't know what John looks like or sounds like. Don't take it unless you HAVE to.

John Craig is simply the most passionate and wise Prof I've ever had during my Post-Secondary journey; there is a lot of reading but it is well worth it because of the passion put into his lecturing. I shared an hour with John outside of Class hours and I left the conversation feeling as if I was 1) respected, 2) appreciated, and 3) more wise.

He is a difficult marker but gives clear and constructive feedback. Very willing to compromise and understanding of students need. He is willing to give a day or two extensions for the essays even if your reasoning is procrastination. Flexible with zoom meetings to help students analyze the harder texts.

I have never had a professor who cared so much about his students doing well. We had a small class size so that may be why. He was there to make sure you did well and we did some really interesting things. He would help with assignments and was always accessible through email and gave good feedback. The assignments are all straightforward.

He said the course was going to be about Britian at War. I took the class for my interest in Britian but all we talked about was some diarist from the 1940's. By far one of the worst classes I've taken. Taking notes is hard because mid lecture he will jump from topic to topic without distinction and be prepared for TONS of reading.

OH MY GOD! I took this class as an elective and it was the worst decision of my life! DO NOT TAKE AS AN ELECTIVE! Crazy amount of readings in old english!!!!! And its so subjective to the TA how well your writing assignments are. It was not worth an elective.
